heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 556 Days: Unconventional Gas Plan, Universal Health Decree, Electoral Reform Passed
heroSubtitle: President Sheinbaum unveiled a major energy sovereignty plan on April 8 to develop Mexico's unconventional (shale) gas deposits, reducing 75% U.S. gas dependence — provoking controversy over a same-day meeting with BlackRock CEO Larry Fink, which she denied influenced the decision. The Sistema Universal de Salud decree was signed, allowing 87 million Mexicans to access any public health facility. The Chamber of Deputies approved the 'Plan B' electoral reform on April 9, completing passage. The ANTAC transporters' megablockade in 20 states was temporarily paused after a 'responsible agreement' with state officials. The peso strengthened to ~17.80 MXN/USD as Mexico's USMCA exemption from 'Liberation Day 2.0' global tariffs held firm.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español, Lopez Doriga
